title: n8n Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**n8n** is a self-hostable / Cloud workflow-automation platform. Each Shielva tenant runs their own n8n workspace and exposes its REST API at `${instance_url}/api/v1`. This connector — `N8nConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "n8n"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Workflows | `/workflows` | List, get, create, update, delete, activate, deactivate, transfer |
| Executions | `/executions` | List, get, delete past runs |
| Credentials | `/credentials` | List, get, create, delete provider credentials (metadata) |
| Tags | `/tags` | List + create tags for workflow grouping |
| Users | `/users` | List instance users (community / enterprise) |
| Variables | `/variables` | List instance-level environment variables (enterprise) |

The connector normalises workflows + executions into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`) when `sync()` is invoked, surfaces 16 standalone `async def` methods per provider operation (OCP), retries 429 / 5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (max 3) honouring `Retry-After`, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::N8nHTTPClient`).

**Per-tenant base URL.** Unlike a fixed-API provider, n8n is *tenant-hosted*. `instance_url` is a mandatory install field and `${instance_url}/api/v1` is computed at `__init__` — there is no class-level constant base URL.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Mint an n8n API key

1. Sign in to your n8n workspace — Cloud (`https://yourorg.app.n8n.cloud`) or self-hosted.
2. Open **Settings → n8n API** (gear icon, bottom-left).
3. Click **Create an API key**, give it a label, copy the value. n8n displays the key only once — rotate if lost.
4. Note your workspace URL — the part before `/workflow/*`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → n8n**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `instance_url` (text) | Your workspace base, e.g. `https://acme.app.n8n.cloud` |
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |
| `timeout_s` (number) | Per-request timeout, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the two required fields are non-empty and initialises the `N8nHTTPClient`. It does NOT call the API.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` immediately after install, which lists 1 workflow as a lightweight probe:

- 2xx → `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`
- 401 → `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` (key revoked or wrong)
- 403 → `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` (key lacks permissions)
- 429 → `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)` with a structured-log warning
- 5xx / transport → `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)`

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
n8n REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry by default — tokens are revoked manually in the n8n UI.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `X-N8N-API-KEY` header verbatim. **No `Authorization` header is used** — that's a Wix/Slack convention, not n8n's.
- `instance_url` determines the per-tenant base URL `${instance_url}/api/v1`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `instance_url` + `api_key`. Initialises `N8nHTTPClient`. Does NOT call any n8n endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=self.api_key, token_type="api_key")` for surface compatibility — no exchange occurs. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /workflows?limit=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request to `${instance_url}/api/v1/*`:

```
X-N8N-API-KEY: <api_key>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `instance_url` | text | yes | `self.instance_url` | Per-tenant n8n base URL |
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `X-N8N-API-KEY` header value |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.timeout_s` (default 30) | Per-request httpx timeout |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE       = "n8n"
AUTH_TYPE            = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["instance_url", "api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

### Base URL computation

```python
# In connector.py
self.base_url = self._compute_base_url(self.instance_url)
# https://acme.app.n8n.cloud      → https://acme.app.n8n.cloud/api/v1
# https://acme.app.n8n.cloud/     → https://acme.app.n8n.cloud/api/v1
# https://acme.app.n8n.cloud/api/v1 → https://acme.app.n8n.cloud/api/v1  (idempotent)
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `N8nConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::N8nHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validates `instance_url` + `api_key`; initialises `N8nHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns `TokenInfo(access_token=self.api_key, token_type="api_key")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 workflow via `GET /workflows?limit=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates workflows + executions via cursor pagination → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Workflows
children.content:
### `async list_workflows(*, active=None, tags=None, name=None, project_id=None, exclude_pinned_data=False, limit=100, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /workflows` with optional camelCase query params (`active`, `tags`, `name`, `projectId`, `excludePinnedData`, `limit`, `cursor`).

### `async get_workflow(workflow_id, *, exclude_pinned_data=False) -> Dict`
`GET /workflows/{id}`.

### `async create_workflow(name, nodes, connections, settings=None, static_data=None) -> Dict`
`POST /workflows` — body: `{name, nodes, connections, settings?, staticData?}`.

### `async update_workflow(workflow_id, *, name=None, nodes=None, connections=None, settings=None, active=None) -> Dict`
`PUT /workflows/{id}` — only the kwargs that are not `None` go into the body.

### `async delete_workflow(workflow_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /workflows/{id}`.

### `async activate_workflow(workflow_id) -> Dict`
`POST /workflows/{id}/activate`.

### `async deactivate_workflow(workflow_id) -> Dict`
`POST /workflows/{id}/deactivate`.

### `async transfer_workflow(workflow_id, destination_project_id) -> Dict`
`PUT /workflows/{id}/transfer` — body: `{destinationProjectId}`. Enterprise.

children.title: Executions
children.content:
### `async list_executions(*, workflow_id=None, status=None, limit=100, cursor=None, include_data=False) -> Dict`
`GET /executions` with optional camelCase query params (`workflowId`, `status`, `limit`, `cursor`, `includeData`).

### `async get_execution(execution_id, *, include_data=False) -> Dict`
`GET /executions/{id}` — pass `include_data=True` to attach run output.

### `async delete_execution(execution_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /executions/{id}`.

children.title: Credentials
children.content:
### `async list_credentials(*, limit=100, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /credentials` — metadata only (n8n never returns credential `data` on list).

### `async get_credential(credential_id) -> Dict`
`GET /credentials/{id}`.

### `async create_credential(name, type, data) -> Dict`
`POST /credentials` — body: `{name, type, data}`. `data` is the provider-specific field bag (e.g. `{accessToken: "xoxb-..."}` for `slackApi`).

### `async delete_credential(credential_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /credentials/{id}`.

children.title: Tags · Users · Variables
children.content:
### `async list_tags(*, limit=100, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /tags`.

### `async create_tag(name) -> Dict`
`POST /tags` — body: `{name}`.

### `async list_users(*, limit=100, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /users` — community/enterprise.

### `async list_variables(*, limit=100, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /variables` — enterprise.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
N8nError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── N8nAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── N8nBadRequestError                # 400
├── N8nNotFound                       # 404 (alias N8nNotFoundError)
├── N8nConflictError                  # 409
├── N8nRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
├── N8nServerError                    # 5xx
├── N8nNetworkError                   # transport-level after retries exhausted
└── N8nAPIError                       # catch-all for unmapped 4xx
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 404 / 409 | Raise typed exception immediately |
| 401 / 403 | Raise `N8nAuthError` immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header when present; otherwise exponential backoff `min(1s * 2^attempt + jitter, 32s)`; retry up to 3, then raise `N8nRateLimitError` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff; retry up to 3, then raise typed error |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `NetworkError` / `RemoteProtocolError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `N8nNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)        — structured log warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

Direct method calls (e.g. `connector.list_workflows()`) **do not** swallow typed exceptions — callers receive them as-is so the platform layer can decide whether to retry, surface to the user, or quarantine.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `instance_url` or `api_key` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. n8n displays the key only once at creation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from n8n — API key disabled or wrong scope | In n8n → Settings → n8n API, verify the key is enabled. Community-edition keys lack `/variables` and some `/users` endpoints — that's expected. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key revoked or `instance_url` wrong | Verify the workspace URL matches `https://<workspace>.app.n8n.cloud` (Cloud) or your self-hosted hostname. Re-create the API key if it was deleted. |
| `n8n.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (n8n per-instance rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| `Network error: Connection refused` | Self-hosted n8n not reachable from gateway | Verify the gateway's egress allowlist (see `vault_egress_allowlist_prod_requirement.md`) and confirm the n8n host is publicly resolvable / VPC-routable. |
| `404 Not Found: Workflow not found` on `get_workflow` | The workflow id was deleted between list + get | Re-list to get current ids. n8n IDs are stable per-workspace; cross-workspace IDs do NOT match. |
| `400 Bad Request: nodes must be an array` on `create_workflow` | Required fields missing | Always pass `name`, `nodes` (list), `connections` (dict). `settings` and `staticData` are optional. |
| `409 Conflict: Tag already exists` on `create_tag` | Duplicate tag name | n8n tags are unique per-instance. Use `list_tags` to check existence before create. |
| `transfer_workflow` returns 403 | Enterprise-only endpoint | The `/transfer` endpoint requires an Enterprise n8n license. Community workspaces will 403. |
| `list_variables` / `list_users` returns 403 | Community-edition restriction | Both endpoints exist on Enterprise; some are gated to admin/owner users on Community. |
| The connector imports but `from connector import N8nConnector` fails in a fresh repl | `sys.path` not bootstrapped | The package `__init__.py` self-bootstraps both the connector root and `shielva-connectors/core`. If you're running from outside the package, set `SHIELVA_CONNECTORS_CORE=/path/to/core` env var. |
